english: discourse, speech, conversation; W.: *ldá-gu tàń-cè* (*lda gu tang ce*) to speak; *ldá-gu sè-cè med* (*lda gu shes ce med*) one cannot understand what is spoken or said; lda-gu-can (lda gu can) talkative Cs.
Ld.-Glr. dha-mán (dha man), a couple of small kettle-drums, one hanging in front, the other behind, the latter being beaten by a second person that follows the bearer.
a kind of ornament of silk or cotton, a fringe or tassel, dár-gyi (dar gyi), rín-po-čei (rin po che'i), esp. worn in sacrificing, Lex.

pf. bldags (bldags), fut. bldag (bldag), imp. ldog (ldog), to lick, krag (khrag) blood; klad ldág-pa (klad ldag pa) the brain being licked up, a punishment of hell Thgy.; ńá-bza-la (na bza' la), or -nas (-nas) to lick a person's coat Mil.; *ldag-ldog* (*ldag ldog*) W. = pe-srul (pe srul), lit. 'a lick', i.e. a pap prepared of rtsám-pa (rtsam pa) and čań (chang), licked from the fingers, or eaten with a spoon.

1. v. ldáń-ba (ldang ba). — 2. for ydań (gdang) stand, frame, trestle. — 3. W. *ldań-ldań-la kur* (*ldang ldang la khur*) carry it lengthways! opp. to pŕed (phred); *ldań-ldań-la dád-cè* (*ldang ldang la dad ce*) to rock with one's chair.
the yarn-beam of a loom Sch.

Ssk. शरभ (śarabha), Fouc. Gyatch. 282; if the text is correct, it would seem preferable to connect ri-dags (ri dags) with ldań-sgo-ska (ldang sgo ska), and to render it: 'the animal Sarabha', a fabulous eight-footed creature of the snowy mountains.

pf. ldańs (ldangs) or lańs (langs), imp. ldoń (ldong), 1. vb. n. to sláń-ba (slang ba), to rise, to get up (cf. the more frq. secondary form láń-ba (lang ba)), °gyél-ba-las (°gyel ba las) from a fall Wdń.; nyál-las (nyal las) from a lying position Lex.; stán-las (stan las) from a seat; to-ráńs (tho rangs) in the morning Lt.; ńó-mi-šes-pa-la (ngo mi shes pa la) before, or in presence of a stranger; also used of the bristling of the hair, Lt., of the rising of vapours, perfumes, dust, of a wind springing up; to extend, to spread, dri ńán-pa pýogs bčur ldań (dri ngan pa phyogs bcur ldang) an offensive smell is spreading in every quarter Tar.; °khrúgs-pa dbús-nas (°khrugs pa dbus nas) the rebellion (spread) from the province of Ü, Ma.; to break out, mé-ro ldań (me ro ldang) the smothered flame breaks out again; in a special sense of morbid matter that has accumulated (gsóg-pa (gsog pa)) Med. frq., e.g. k̭a-zás zú-nas ldań (kha zas zhu nas ldang) during digestion the symptoms break out anew; dgrá-ru ldáń-ba (dgra ru ldang ba) to show one's self an enemy, to break out into hostilities frq.; to arise, originate, break out, of disease, despair, Mil.; also for: to have risen, to stand, but only in certain combinations, ldań dub byéd-pa (ldang dub byed pa) tired from having been standing (so long) Lt. — 2. W. to suffice, to be sufficient, enough (cf. loń-ba (long ba)) = k̭yéd-pa (khyed pa), of food, clothes, money; hence ldań (ldang): complete, perfect, entire, whole, *ras náń-ša rág-ma gos ldań čig* (*ras nang sha rag ma gos ldang cig*) cotton cloth with lining (sufficient) for a whole dress; *dú-gu gos ldań nyis* (*du gu gos ldang nyis*) woolen yarn for two complete dresses. — ldań (ldang) prob. signifies also quite through, cf. ltań (ltang) II.; ldań-tsád (ldang tshad) occurs in medical works, and in many cases seems to imply quantity; neu-ldań (ne'u ldang) Lex. = na-mnyám (na mnyam) of the same age (Sch. not corr.).

1. vb. pf. and fut. bldad (bldad), imp. ldod (ldod), to chew Zam., W.; skyug-ldád (skyug ldad) Cs., v. skyug bldeg-čiń ldad-pa (skyug bldeg cing ldad pa) (?) Sch. to chew the cud, to ruminate; log (log) Cs. 1. id., 2. rumination, deliberate reflection; Pur.: *spá ldad-čes* (*spa ldad ces*) to taste, to try; Ld.: *di ldad-če* (*di ldad ce*) to smell at. — 2. Ld. for glád-pa (glad pa)

I. sbst., also mdán-pa (mdan pa) Lex., cheek, ldán(-pai) so (ldan(-pa'i) so) cheek-tooth, molar tooth; ldan-lcàg (ldan lcag) Cs. a blow on the cheek, a box on the ear; *dẹ́n-tsóg* (*den tsog*) C. id.; *mi dhé-la dẹ́n-tsog gyag (or gyab) soń, mi dhe dẹ́n-tsog-gḥi máń-po duń soń* (*mi dhe la den tsog rgyag (or rgyab) song, mi dhe den tsog ghi mang po rdung song*) his ears have been soundly boxed; metaph. grog-ldán (grog ldan) the cheek or side of a ravine Mil. nt. II. vb. and adj. 1. originally: to be near to, hard by, a thing, (juxta), hence W. *ldán-la (ldan la), ldán-du* (*ldan du*), adv. and postp., near to, by, *ńẹ ldán-la dug* (*nge ldan la dug*) sit down by my side; *śiń-gi ldán-du* (*shing gi ldan du*) close by the tree; *ńai ldán-du šog* (*nga'i ldan du shog*) come near to me! *gám-mi ldán-du* (*gam mi ldan du*) near the box; *tser-máń-ńi ldán-la ḍúl-čè* (*tsher mang ngi ldan la 'dul ce*) to go along the side of a hedge. — 2, in B. and C. only <
